data.text: The origin and historical development of a linguistic form as shown by determining its basic elements, earliest known use, and changes in form and meaning, tracing its transmission from one language to another, identifying its cognates in other languages, and reconstructing its ancestral form where possible.
data.attributionText: from The American Heritage® Dictionary of the English Language, 5th Edition.

data.text: The branch of linguistics that deals with etymologies.
data.attributionText: from The American Heritage® Dictionary of the English Language, 5th Edition.

data.text: That part of philology which treats of the history of words in respect both to form and to meanings, tracing them back toward their origin, and setting forth and explaining the changes they have undergone.
Specifically The particular history of a word, including an account of its various forms and senses.
In grammar, that division of grammar which treats of the parts of speech and their inflections.
That branch of philological science which treats of the history of words, tracing out their origin, primitive significance, and changes of form and meaning.
data.attributionText: from the GNU version of the Collaborative International Dictionary of English.

data.text: That part of grammar which relates to the changes in the form of the words in a language; inflection.
data.attributionText: from the GNU version of the Collaborative International Dictionary of English.

data.text: The study of the <xref>historical</xref> development of languages, particularly as manifested in individual words.
data.attributionText: from Wiktionary, Creative Commons Attribution/Share-Alike License.

data.text: An account of the <xref>origin</xref> and <xref>historical</xref> development of a word.
data.attributionText: from Wiktionary, Creative Commons Attribution/Share-Alike License.

data.text: the study of the sources and development of words
data.attributionText: from WordNet 3.0 Copyright 2006 by Princeton University. All rights reserved.
